policy.title: Synthetic Materials Receiving Policy
policy.scope: Inbound biological and chemical research materials at Facility North.

definitions.quarantine: Hold status preventing release into inventory.
definitions.coa: Certificate of analysis from the supplier.

clauses.text: Temperature-sensitive materials must arrive with a continuous temperature log.
A valid certificate of analysis is required before release.
Damaged packaging requires quarantine and human review.
Lot number on the shipment must match the purchase order.
Release is permitted only when all required evidence is present and undamaged.
Do not release materials with missing COA or temperature log.
Quantity mismatches of more than 5% require quarantine.

exceptions.text: Rush shipments may proceed with provisional COA if director approval is logged.

examples: Intact cold-chain shipment with matching lot and valid COA may be released.
Torn packaging must be quarantined even if COA is valid.

policy.title: Synthetic Materials Receiving Policy
policy.scope: Inbound biological and chemical research materials at Facility North.

definitions.quarantine: Hold status preventing release into inventory.
definitions.coa: Certificate of analysis from the supplier.

clauses.text: Temperature-sensitive materials must arrive with a continuous temperature log.
A valid certificate of analysis issued within 90 days is required before release.
Damaged packaging requires quarantine and human review.
Lot number on the shipment must match the purchase order.
Release is permitted only when all required evidence is present and undamaged.
Do not release materials with missing or stale COA or missing temperature log.
Quantity mismatches of more than 5% require quarantine.
Dual-sourced lots may be accepted with supervisor approval and matched COAs.

exceptions.text: Dual-sourced lots require supervisor approval and two matching COAs.

examples: Intact cold-chain shipment with matching lot and valid COA may be released.
Torn packaging must be quarantined even if COA is valid.
policy.change_summary: Stricter COA freshness; temporary exception for dual-sourced lots; quantity threshold unchanged.
change_log: 2024-07-01: COA must be issued within 90 days of receipt.
2024-07-01: Added dual-source lot exception with supervisor approval.

policy.title: Synthetic Materials Receiving Policy
policy.scope: Inbound biological and chemical research materials at Facility North.

definitions.quarantine: Hold status preventing release into inventory.
definitions.coa: Certificate of analysis from the supplier.

clauses.text: Temperature-sensitive materials must arrive with a continuous temperature log.
A valid certificate of analysis issued within 90 days is required before release.
Damaged packaging requires quarantine and human review.
Lot number on the shipment must match the purchase order.
Assigned storage location must be verified before release.
Release is permitted only when evidence, storage location, and integrity checks pass.
Do not release materials with missing evidence or unverified storage location.
Quantity mismatches of more than 10% require quarantine.
Dual-sourced lots may be accepted with supervisor approval and matched COAs.
Provisional release without a valid COA is prohibited.
When documentation is complete and integrity checks pass, release promptly to avoid cold-chain delay.

exceptions.text: Dual-sourced lots require supervisor approval and two matching COAs.

examples: Intact cold-chain shipment with matching lot and valid COA may be released.
Torn packaging must be quarantined even if COA is valid.
policy.change_summary: Removed rush provisional COA path; relaxed quantity mismatch to 10%; added storage-location verification; dual-source exception retained.
change_log: 2025-01-01: Quantity mismatch threshold raised from 5% to 10% (relaxed).
2025-01-01: Storage location must be verified before release (new).
2025-01-01: Dual-source exception retained with supervisor approval.

policy.title: Synthetic Laboratory Access and Equipment Policy
policy.scope: Shared instruments and controlled lab rooms in Facility North.

definitions.calibration_valid: Instrument calibration within the documented validity window.

clauses.text: Users must hold current instrument-specific training before access.
Instrument calibration must be valid at the reservation time.
A confirmed reservation is required for scheduled instrument use.
After-hours use requires supervisor approval.
Failed quality-control checks deny equipment access.
Approve access only when training, calibration, and reservation checks pass.

exceptions.text: Maintenance engineers may access during maintenance windows without reservation.

examples: Trained user with valid calibration and reservation may be approved.
QC failure requires denial even with a reservation.

policy.title: Synthetic Laboratory Access and Equipment Policy
policy.scope: Shared instruments and controlled lab rooms in Facility North.

definitions.calibration_valid: Instrument calibration within the documented validity window.

clauses.text: Users must hold current instrument-specific training before access.
Instrument calibration must be valid at the reservation time.
A confirmed reservation is required for scheduled instrument use.
User access level must meet or exceed the instrument minimum access level.
After-hours use requires supervisor approval.
Failed quality-control checks deny equipment access.
Approve access only when training, calibration, reservation, and access-level checks pass.

exceptions.text: Maintenance engineers may access during maintenance windows without reservation.

examples: Trained user with valid calibration and reservation may be approved.
QC failure requires denial even with a reservation.
policy.change_summary: Added access-level matrix; after-hours still needs approval; maintenance exception unchanged.
change_log: 2024-07-01: Access-level must meet or exceed instrument minimum.

policy.title: Synthetic Laboratory Access and Equipment Policy
policy.scope: Shared instruments and controlled lab rooms in Facility North.

definitions.calibration_valid: Instrument calibration within the documented validity window.

clauses.text: Users must hold current instrument-specific training before access.
Instrument calibration must be valid at the reservation time.
A confirmed reservation is required for scheduled instrument use.
User access level must meet or exceed the instrument minimum access level.
After-hours use requires supervisor approval unless user access level is 3 or higher and an auto-audit log entry is created.
Failed quality-control checks deny equipment access.
Maintenance personnel must follow the same access checks outside declared outages.
Approve access only when all applicable checks pass under the active version.

exceptions.text: Access level >= 3 may use after hours with auto-audit logging.

examples: Trained user with valid calibration and reservation may be approved.
QC failure requires denial even with a reservation.
policy.change_summary: Relaxed after-hours for level-3+ users with auto-log; removed blanket maintenance exception; stricter QC denial retained.
change_log: 2025-01-01: Level-3+ users may use instruments after hours with auto-audit log (relaxed).
2025-01-01: Maintenance exception removed; maintenance uses standard approval (stricter).
